title: NASA reveals its Roman Space Telescope today: How to watch live, and what's next for the next-generation observatory
summary: NASA is set to reveal the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope on April 21, with a live event available to watch on Space.com. The next-generation observatory aims to advance astronomical research and discoveries.

title: NASA's Curiosity rover finds building blocks of life on Mars. Scientists aren't sure how they got there
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover discovered organic molecules on Mars, which are considered building blocks for life. Scientists are uncertain about the origin of these molecules.

title: NASA shuts down Voyager 1 instrument to keep probe exploring interstellar space
summary: NASA has shut down another instrument on Voyager 1 to preserve its power, ensuring the probe continues exploring interstellar space. This decision is part of ongoing efforts to extend the spacecraft's operational lifespan as it travels farther from Earth.

title: 600 rocket landings! SpaceX notches another milestone during Sunday Starlink launch (video)
summary: SpaceX achieved its 600th successful landing of a Falcon first stage during a Starlink mission launched on April 19. The Falcon 9 rocket carried 25 Starlink satellites from Vandenberg Space Force Station.

title: Scientists found a 'bathtub ring' on Mars. Could it be evidence of an ancient Red Planet ocean?
summary: Scientists discovered a geological feature on Mars resembling a 'bathtub ring,' suggesting it may be evidence of an ancient ocean covering a third of the planet. This finding could provide insights into Mars' past water history and potential habitability.

title: Astronauts face grip strength challenge in space and on Earth, scientists find
summary: Scientists discovered that living in space can permanently affect brain function, including grip strength challenges for astronauts on Earth and in space.

title: Watch the Lyrid meteor shower 2026 online today with these free livestreams
summary: The Lyrid meteor shower in 2026 can be watched online through free livestreams, allowing viewers to observe the event from home. Global night sky cameras provide access to the celestial phenomenon.

title: A peek at the far side of the moon | Space photo of the day for April 20, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts captured a unique image of the moon's 'dark side' during their historic April 2026 flyby. This marks a significant milestone in lunar exploration.

title: '80s sci-fi cult classic 'The Last Starfighter' finally scores a sequel, but it's a comic book! (exclusive)
summary: The 1980s sci-fi cult classic 'The Last Starfighter' is getting a sequel in the form of a comic book. The new project aims to continue the adventure for a new generation of fans.

title: A School of Mud Volcano Islands in Azerbaijan
summary: Azerbaijan's Absheron Peninsula features tadpole-shaped islands formed by explosive mud volcano eruptions and reshaped by erosion. These unique geological formations highlight the dynamic natural processes in the region.

title: NASA on Track for Future Missions with Initial Artemis II Assessments
summary: NASA has completed initial assessments following the successful Artemis II mission splashdown, analyzing data from the Orion spacecraft, SLS rocket, and launch pad systems. The mission is on track for future lunar exploration efforts.

title: NASA Rolls Out Artemis III Moon Rocket Core Stage
summary: NASA rolled out the core stage of the SLS rocket for the Artemis III Moon mission, scheduled for 2027, following the successful Artemis II test flight.

title: NASA Invites Media to SpaceX’s 34th Resupply Launch to Space Station
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for SpaceX's 34th resupply mission to the International Space Station. The launch, using SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket, will deliver science investigations, supplies, and equipment.

title: NASA Welcomes Latvia as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Latvia signed the Artemis Accords, becoming the 62nd nation to commit to responsible space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Latvia's participation in the accords.

title: Wheels Up for X-59
summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft achieved a key milestone by flying with wheels up, marking a significant step in its test campaign. The experimental aircraft has also completed its highest and fastest flights to date, expanding its operational range.

title: Widely Attended Gatherings (WAGs) Determinations
summary: The document lists various space industry events and gatherings scheduled for 2026, including the 41st Space Symposium, Space Policy Institute events, and awards ceremonies hosted by organizations like The Planetary Society and AAU.

title: NASA’s Hubble Dazzles With Young Stars in Trifid Nebula
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a detailed image of the Trifid Nebula, showcasing a region of star formation 5,000 light-years from Earth. The image, taken in visible light, marks the 36th anniversary of Hubble's launch.

title: Thailand’s Krabi Coast
summary: Thailand's Krabi Coast is known for its tropical karst landscapes and sandy beaches. The region features a mix of natural and developed areas.

title: CSDA Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Satellogic NewSat Data
summary: The CSDA Quality Assessment Report has evaluated Satellogic's NewSat data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research and applications. This adds to the growing documentation of commercial data's role in scientific studies.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: A busy week in space launches is expected with seven missions planned, involving four countries and five different launch vehicles. The missions include GPS, Progress, and Starlink deployments.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a failed launch on Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for the company's planned space-based cellular broadband network.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time. This marks a significant milestone in the rocket's partially reusable design.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a large docking adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. This innovation supports the development of commercial space stations.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fires as part of the Artemis program. This marks a significant milestone for the upcoming missions.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew experienced vehicle departures and a spacewalk in March. This marked a busy month aboard the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are planned globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool completing its CRS NG-23 mission.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. The mission marked a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session in April-May and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026. The forum aims to gather feedback from small- and medium-sized enterprises on topics affecting their work in the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully placed in an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the navigation technology.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved precision or efficiency in directional control. It highlights innovations that could enhance mission performance or reduce operational costs.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that microgravity significantly enhances the propulsion effect of lasers on graphene aerogels.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment simulating space conditions on a rollercoaster demonstrated this potential.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that can perform multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space technology. The innovation is highlighted as a breakthrough in the industry.

title: Opportunities Beyond the Moon Opened by CLPS
summary: NASA plans to launch monthly uncrewed missions to the Moon starting next year under the CLPS program, creating opportunities for various space industry companies beyond just lunar-focused ones.

title: NASA’s 2028 Moon Landing Has A Spacesuit Problem, New Report Says
summary: NASA's planned 2028 Moon landing faces delays due to issues with spacesuit development, as reported by the Office of the Inspector General (OIG). The setback threatens the agency's timeline for returning astronauts to the lunar surface.

title: Latvia To Join Artemis Accords Today
summary: Latvia is set to join the Artemis Accords today, marking a significant step in international space cooperation. The move aligns with global efforts to establish norms of behavior and contribute to NASA's Artemis program.

title: SpaceX Won A Mars Mission That Might Get Cancelled
summary: SpaceX has been awarded a contract to launch the ESA Rosalind Franklin rover to Mars as early as 2028 for $175.7M. However, NASA's potential cancellation of its participation in the mission, as suggested by the White House's FY2027 budget request, casts uncertainty on the project's future.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is expanding into the thruster market, aiming to vertically integrate and address scale concerns for customers. The company's CFO highlighted the need for large-scale production capabilities in the industry.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is preparing its first qualification spacesuit for a 2027 flight, as confirmed by Chief Astronaut Michael López-Alegría. The company is advancing suit production despite external uncertainties.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS). Axiom Space was previously the sole provider for NASA's Private Astronaut Missions (PAMs), but new competitors like Voyager Technologies are entering the market by 2026.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has formed a new advisory council to address key industry challenges such as sustainability, regulatory hurdles, national security, and international collaboration. The initiative aims to shape strategic direction for the space sector.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: ORBX ETF launches to track companies earning over half their revenue from space tech, including transportation, software, and satellite services. This follows a wave of space tech IPOs in the industry.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test the agency's 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to demonstrate advanced space-based communication capabilities.

title: Poland Invests in New Defense GEO Satellite Built by Thales, Airbus, and Radmor
summary: Poland has signed a contract with Thales, Airbus, and Radmor to develop a new defense GEO satellite, enhancing its space defense capabilities. This investment marks Poland's latest move to strengthen its position in European space security.

title: New Glenn Grounded and AST SpaceMobile Satellite Lost After Launch Anomaly
summary: New Glenn rocket was grounded after a launch anomaly caused AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite to be placed into the wrong orbit, resulting in its loss. AST SpaceMobile plans to file an insurance claim for the failed satellite.
company: Blue Origin (New Glenn) and AST SpaceMobile

title: Ovzon Launches New Mobile Terminal in Small Form Factor
summary: Ovzon, a Swedish satellite operator, has launched a new mobile satellite terminal called the Ovzon T8 in a small form factor. The terminal is designed for mobility across air, land, and sea.

title: Inside SatEnlight’s Spiral Staircase of Lasers: Interview with Startup Space Winner Matteo Vismara
summary: SatEnlight, an optical communications technology company based in Milan, Italy, uses a unique multi-layered laser beam arrangement in its terminals. The company was featured as a Startup Space winner in an interview.

title: IFC News Roundup from AIX
summary: The AIX Aircraft Interiors Expo highlighted satellite companies advocating for in-flight connectivity (IFC) solutions to airlines. Key announcements were featured in the IFC News Roundup from the event.

title: Airlines See IFC as Way to Build Relationships With Customers
summary: Airlines are leveraging in-flight connectivity (IFC) to enhance customer relationships as it becomes a key part of the flying experience. The focus on IFC is highlighted as a strategy to improve passenger engagement.

title: NorthStar Earth & Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal at $300M Valuation
summary: NorthStar Earth & Space plans to go public via a SPAC deal valued at $300 million. The Canadian space domain awareness company integrates ground-based systems.

title: ESA Taps Kepler to Lead Next Stage of HydRON Optical Program
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Kepler Communications as the prime contractor for HydRON Element 3, the third phase of a sovereign optical communications constellation. This marks a significant step in advancing optical communication technology for space applications.

title: Space Force, NATO Commanders Share Threat Perspectives
summary: U.S. Space Force is actively identifying new space capabilities and managing threats from adversaries while creating missions to defend assets. NATO commanders share perspectives on space security threats.

title: Air Force Awarded Development Contract for Space-Based AMTI; Meink Says Risk Low
summary: The Department of the Air Force awarded a contract for space-based air moving target indication (AMTI) development to multiple vendors. Meink stated that the risk associated with the project is low.
